mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate directly impacts equity discount rates. A persistently high FEDFUNDS rate implies elevated cost of capital, compressing valuation multiples and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level against 20-day forward returns remains positive or below -0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or t-test of mean returns in high vs low rate environments yields p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals a weakening labor market that will reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5% from below, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative at the 5% level over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation (CPIAUCSL above 310) and high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS above 5%) create a toxic combination of compressed margins and elevated discount rates, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within a week as markets reprice risk.
failure_condition: Cumulative return over 5-day windows following joint trigger events is positive or average return is less negative than -1% over a 3-year period; or joint trigger events produce fewer than 10 instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL > 300), further increases in the CPI index confirm persistent price pressures that force the Fed to maintain or raise rates, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL is above 300, the rank IC of (CPIAUCSL, 20d forward return) is positive or above -0.03; or during these regimes the mean forward return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A steadily expanding payroll count (PAYEMS) signals broad-based economic vigor, rising aggregate demand, and improving corporate earnings, which consistently lifts the S&P 500 over a quarterly horizon irrespective of other conditions.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of PAYEMS and 60-day forward return is negative over any 5-year window, or the mean return after PAYEMS increases is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 100 (the 2017 baseline), it signals a contraction in manufacturing activity that leads to lower corporate profits and economic slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When INDPRO drops below 100, the average 20-day SPX return is positive, or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 55% over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong concurrent momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals overheating consumer demand that will trigger policy tightening and raise recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: 6-month growth rate of RSAFS > 5% and HOUST > 1500
failure_condition: When both RSAFS momentum and HOUST exceed triggers, the average 60-day forward return is not negative, or the signal generates fewer than 5 instances in a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where UNRATE is below 4%, further declines in unemployment exacerbate wage pressures and force the Fed to tighten policy more aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 4%, the rank correlation between (UNRATE, 20d forward return) is positive or above -0.03; or the mean forward return during such periods is not negative.
